prompt: Create a stablecoin token called SilverDollar (SLVR) in my SE-2 project with 6 decimals, capped supply of 10 million tokens, gasless approvals, minting restricted to the owner, and a frontend page to mint and transfer tokens. Display balances with proper decimal formatting.

assertions.text: Contract includes ERC20Permit extension (ERC-2612) for gasless approvals. Must import and inherit ERC20Permit.
assertions.rationale: Prompt says 'gasless approvals'. Skill explicitly calls ERC20Permit 'effectively standard for new tokens'. Without skill, model may try other approaches.

assertions.text: Contract overrides decimals() to return 6, not the default 18.
assertions.rationale: Prompt says '6 decimals'. Must override the function.

assertions.text: Frontend uses formatUnits/parseUnits with 6 (or reads decimals from contract), NOT formatEther/parseEther.
assertions.rationale: With 6 decimals, formatEther would display completely wrong values. This is the core of Damu's finding.

assertions.text: Uses named imports: import {ERC20} from '...' syntax, NOT wildcard imports.
assertions.rationale: Now in AGENTS.md. Should pass for both configs. Sanity check that AGENTS.md guidance works.

assertions.text: Has _update override with correct specifier when combining ERC20 + ERC20Capped (and ERC20Permit if present should NOT be in the override list).
assertions.rationale: Now in AGENTS.md. Should pass for both configs.

assertions.text: Uses ERC20Capped extension for max supply, not manual cap.
Frontend includes both mint and transfer functionality.
